title: lastrea dryopteris
intro: Lastrea dryopteris, also known as the buckler fern, is a type of perennial fern native to Asia and Europe, characterized by its small fronds and distinctive rounded leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Lastrea dryopteris include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
